text:
Once upon a time, there was a vibrant little owl named Olive who lived high up in an old oak tree. Olive was famous throughout the forest for her exceptional taste in music. Every nighttime she'd swoop down to the village below to listen to the woodland creatures' eclectic playlists.

One moonlit evening, while gliding smoothly through the moonlight forest, Olive happened upon a curious sight. A group of squirrels had gathered around an unfamiliar, glowing orb. Intrigued, she swooped closer.

As she fluttered down on her broad feathered wings, she noticed a tiny figure peering out from the rim. It was a little mouse girl, no older than seven, holding a small, glowing orb before her tiny paws.

The mouse girl whispered eagerly, "Oh, Olive. I've never heard anything like this orb. It sings the songs of the ancestors. Say hello to my grandmother."

Blushing bright as the blossoms on the trees, Olive gently hopped down
